steps.title: Add the equations to find 8y
steps.content:
Notice that $5(3x)$ and $-5(3x)$ appear in equations 1 and 2 respectively. Adding the equations eliminates the $3x$ term:

$$[5(3x) + 4(8y)] + [-5(3x) + 4(8y)] = 16 + 16$$

$$8(8y) = 32$$
$$8y = 4$$

steps.title: Subtract the equations to find 3x and compute the sum
steps.content:
Subtracting equation 2 from equation 1 eliminates the $8y$ term:

$$[5(3x) + 4(8y)] - [-5(3x) + 4(8y)] = 16 - 16$$

$$10(3x) = 0$$
$$3x = 0$$

Therefore, $3x + 8y = 0 + 4 = 4$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
